entry.title: I vs. AI?
entry.content:
Was talking about it (article: Can AI Agent replace IT Architects (EA/SA)?) – but the topic is just too big (these days?)… There are too many disruptions, not only because AI, but around AI – AI use-caes, controversial water consumption, even locking (or limiting) usage of AI with "decrees". What is next?  Shell, we expect conflicts over AI (or resources needed to power AI Data Centers)?

I guess everything is possible, but aren’t we forgetting something? We are the ones who write the future for the “next” generations… Or we should be the ones who write it!

The plot…

We are already seeing situations, AI is replacing “on masses” many people, in many professions. If we stick to IT, it is well known fact, junior developers have difficult time finding jobs these days – a lot of coding is being done by GenAI (not to go to specific flavors). So, if coders/programs can be “replaced” who else can be replaced?

What about SA or EA?

Of course, an IT Architecture can be observed as a process of reasoning, decision making, and acting accordingly. There is of course a lot of knowledge (experience) and ceremony (frameworks). One may say – nothing we cannot replace with good AI model, RAG and vector database…

While I can argue, things are not so simple (although there is perception, it might be sust so easy), inevitable AI Agents (or Agentic AI) will be put into use to replace IT Architecture work in many aspects. Initially it will come as a “helper” tools (I guess this is how it was “sold” to coders/developers some time ago), then tools will “grow” and become more powerful. At some point of time, our mangers will be confident, AI Agents can take over “bulk” of our work, and start give us more “free” time to work on “other” tasks… Everything else, afterwards – I assume we can guess…

What about “those” managers?

In fact, if we start delegating to AI Agents sensitive Architectural decisions, no doubt senior managers might think of “releasing” some “repetitive” work form the mid-management. I assume building such RAG and vector database might not be so difficult after all…

What about “any” managers?

To go few steps further, why would anyone believe, one day somebody “else” would not build an AI Agent capable of replacing his/her work?

Future is bright?

To go a bit into future (not so distant?), no reason to expect that modern robots and “smart” AI Agents cannot replace majority of current human professions – obviously it’s not only manual factory workers, or various “siting” roles in administration and IT– it could also be lawyers, doctors, teachers… No this is not a trailer for some “B-production” SF movie; this is the reality we are “slowly” rushing into.

Conceptually, what would be the “hold”? Technology will most certainly be available – at least, this would be the perception…

  • Initially it will come as a “assistant” tool (I guess this is how it came to "assist" lectors, coders/developers some time ago).
  • Then tools will evelve and become more powerful.
  • At some point of time, imediate decision makers will see benefits in introducing  more "reliable" tools instead of us. 
  • The rest of the story – I =guess, we all know…

While it will take time this “future” start happening everywhere (it is close, but not “that” close) – road is clear, right?

In this future, if “I” am replaced with “AI” (or robot, in gneral), what will stay (for me)? But, things do not end here…

Problem or…

Things are never simple (as I was already trying to say) – replacing the whole class of professionals (whether those are junior developers or coders) or some other professionals will leave a “gap”…

If fact what will become also clear over time – controlling and auditing of AI will emerge as a major issue.

  • Our current AI hallucinate even now when they are fed and trained with knowledge base we humans produced…
  • Next generation of AI will be trained on knowledge current AI is generating, taking all current hallucinations (we miss to correct) as facts, and start adding some new “hallucinations”. This problem will increase exponentially. New generation of people (in a very near future) will take for granted all these things (many take them for granted even now).

Controlling and auditioning of AI and thus correcting AI “decisions” will become increasingly difficult in future. But. some sort of awareness will (most likely) emerge.

… Or opportunity?

Once we become aware of the problem, that usually creates some sort of reaction. And yes, in a good-old EA fashion, we may actually see this rather as an opportunity to improve (ourselves?):

  • As a result of the accumulated hallucinations – I would not be surprised we start gradually returning to “classical education” (detoxicated from AI and even search engines), first as “fashionable” trend, then as a mainstream need.
  • I expect (or hope), exactly all these problems might lead to creating some completely new professions, which will be very (much) demanded in future – people (in various domains) armed with “classical education”, but also capable to understand and work on improving/correcting AI.
  • Some regulations might also pop-up, as we might simply understand that AI (just like other scientific achievements we did) should not be leading toward ultimate “reshaping” (or some would say even “destruction”) of our society (by replacing all of us, or taking resources from use etc.), but toward improving the society and giving us new opportunities. Who knows – maybe…
  • Or more realistically – we cannot replace “all” (or majority?). Things are very simple – if I do not work, I do not earn, thus I do not spend… Who will “fuel” the economy? 

If fact, all this might not be so bad after all? As an outcome, new generations of human professions might start appearing – streaming toward continuous collaboration with AI on “equal” basis, supporting each other, and being aware of the strength and weakness of the other. This would not be a one-way street (as it seems, it is now) but controlled coexistence.

… Although, it will (most certainly) take time until we reach this level of maturity…

Of course, things might be changed again (dramatically?) with the creation of the Self-Aware AI – I guess, an ultimate goal (some) scientists/investors are looking for…

Prologue

There are many versions of the “future”… We’ve heard all the “great” ones, from various marketing channels, and sometimes based on some real good use-cases (let’s be honest). We’ve heard a lot about all those “bad” ones as well, starting from AI taking too many jobs from people, AI is “this or that”, it “hallucinates too much” etc. We’ve heard a lot about overconsumption of water and energy by AI Data Centers. We’ve heard also about issues (becoming now hot topic as well) on “digital sovereignty” – as “he” who controls AI, controls more than just “tools”…

But we might (try) to see some opportunities as well. After all, in all cases future is bringing changes – no use of complaining, changes are fact. The future is not written – we are writing it now. And how good are we in writing good future, this is perhaps the biggest question… So, we better start thinking what future we want!

If we exclude “great” marketing scenarios, “bad” scenarios (as we just do not want ii?), what do we do to make those “good”, but realistic scenarios, happen? How do we design AI to be really supportive, not destructive and yet we also “earn” something (“the thing” which makes world go ‘round)?

  • Probably we will start by setting some grounding governance rules – yes, governance is the key, and yes, mature IT Architecure has pivotal role in shapping those guardrails; implmenting thoe "guardrails" might be very diffirent story.  
  • AI is an asset, water is resource, as well as people – resources must be on the first place. we do not have to introduce AI into every sphere of our life, at least not now. Let's prioritize, can we start with ourselves? 
  • Democratizing AI by removing governmental controls (easier said than done) – sorry we need suveregn freedoom, not another control mechanism, so something has to be done here as well; maybe more competition, why not? 

I am only sharing my 2-cents… And remember, a pessimist is an optimist with experience…

---

Disclaimer: When I talk about AI, I express only my personal view. I am neither against nor pro AI – I am very much in favor of scientific and engineering progress, but I also call for a caution and proper regulations.

entry.title: Re: Learning token server deprecation (2027)
entry.content:
@EricAbrams thank you for your feedback.
1. we couldn't communicate earlier because the work to adopt OIDC for Learning wasn't completed. This is in part the reason for pushing out the token deprecation and allow more time for our customers to transition. 
The Help document pages have now been updated to suggest both solutions (but at the time it was accurate to recommend oAuth 2.0 only since Learning wasn't yet enhanced to work with OIDC).

2. there is no deprecation announcement on this topic as of yet. But our Platform colleagues have been recommending OIDC for Platform integrations since 2H2024: Introducing API Authentication with IAS as token p... - SAP Community this is the reason for us to recommend moving to OIDC directly for our customers who haven't yet transitioned and avoid repeating the process when the deprecation comes.
